blocks.text: filed it. She said exactly what someone would say. He had been doing this long enough to know those two things weren't always different.
had given it to her. She was in pain. He was reading something real.
held his read and said nothing about it yet.

blocks.speaker: Inspector Davenport
blocks.content: You've talked to most of them today.

blocks.speaker: Inspector Davenport
blocks.content: I'm not arguing. What's your read â€” collectively?

blocks.speaker: Julian Crane
blocks.content: Cade Mercer is hiding something specific. Whether it connects to this I don't know yet. Dean and Sophie are exactly what they look like â€” genuinely devastated. Wren saw something she hasn't said. And Nadia â€”

blocks.speaker: Julian Crane
blocks.content: Nadia is the most controlled person in that house. In a situation designed to break composure.

blocks.speaker: Inspector Davenport
blocks.content: Some people are just built that way.

blocks.speaker: Julian Crane
blocks.content: But she was also the most visibly present person all day Saturday. The most active, most social, most accounted for. And then she was also perfectly still during the argument Saturday night. Not tense. Still.

blocks.speaker: Julian Crane
blocks.content: No. It's a pattern. Evidence comes after.

blocks.prompt: Julian just named Nadia to Davenport. Was that the right move?

options.label: Yes. Davenport needed to know where to look.
Too soon. If he's wrong it costs him Davenport's trust.
